toolUseResult.oldString:
- [ ] [Review][Decision] **`git push origin HEAD:main` semantics for non-main dispatch refs** [release.yaml:143-144] ‚Äî Workflow unconditionally pushes HEAD to `main`. Story 3.2's alpha cut dispatches from `feat/release-workflow`, whose HEAD is NOT a fast-forward of `main` ‚Äî push will be rejected by branch protection (required PR reviews per Epic 1 recon), but only AFTER the immutable tag has been pushed (line 140) and BEFORE npm publish runs (line 146). Result: split-brain (tag exists, main not updated, npm not published). Options: (a) skip main-push for prereleases (alpha/beta/rc) since they shouldn't advance main; (b) skip main-push when `github.ref != refs/heads/main`; (c) keep current behavior and accept that Story 3.2 alpha cut will fail at this step (forcing Story 3.3 to merge first before any cut). Decision is load-bearing for Story 3.2 design.
- [ ] [Review][Decision] **Tag push ordering creates split-brain on downstream failure** [release.yaml:134-155] ‚Äî Current order: commit (local) ‚Üí push tag (immutable, line 140) ‚Üí push main (line 144) ‚Üí npm publish (line 146) ‚Üí GitHub Release (line 158). If main-push fails, tag exists pointing to a commit only on the feature branch. If publish fails, tag+main-push succeeded but no npm artifact. Recovery is manual (delete tag, revert commit, re-run). Standard alternative: publish-first-then-tag ‚Äî but conflicts with `npm publish`'s expectation that the tag exists. Options: (a) reorder to publish‚Üítag‚Üímain-push, deleting tag on publish failure; (b) keep current order and document recovery procedure in `docs/RELEASING.md ¬ß Rollback Playbook` (Story 4.1's surface); (c) add an explicit `--dry-run` step before tag push to catch most-likely failures early. Coupled to D1 above.
- [ ] [Review][Decision] **`npm version prerelease` cannot start a new major prerelease cycle** [release.yaml:70-75] ‚Äî Current bump logic: `alpha|beta|rc ‚Üí npm version prerelease --preid=...`. From `0.10.0`, this produces `0.10.1-alpha.0` ‚Äî NOT `1.0.0-alpha.0`. To cut `v1.0.0-rc.1` (Story 5.2's plan), Armel would need to first run `version_bump: major` (producing `1.0.0` immediately, polluting the latest tag) and then... no clean path. Options: (a) accept current limitation, document that Story 5.2 hand-bumps to `1.0.0-rc.0` before dispatching as `rc`; (b) extend `version_bump` choices with `premajor-alpha`, `premajor-beta`, `premajor-rc`; (c) add an `initial_version` workflow input to override. Story 5.2 will hit this ‚Äî flagging now so Story 5.1 readiness audit can decide.

input.old_string: - **`npm install -g npm@latest` is unpinned ‚Äî future npm major can break OIDC contract silently** ‚Äî `.github/workflows/release.yaml:52`. AC #4 / FR13 specifies "npm CLI ‚â• 11.5.1" but the implementation pins to `latest`. Matches the research doc worked example, so consistent with the design baseline. A future `npm@12.x` with breaking changes to OIDC trusted-publishing surface would silently break the next release. Patch-grade fix is `npm@^11.5.1`. Revisit post-v1.0.0 alongside the broader supply-chain hardening pass.
input.new_string:
- **`npm install -g npm@latest` is unpinned ‚Äî future npm major can break OIDC contract silently** ‚Äî `.github/workflows/release.yaml:52`. AC #4 / FR13 specifies "npm CLI ‚â• 11.5.1" but the implementation pins to `latest`. Matches the research doc worked example, so consistent with the design baseline. A future `npm@12.x` with breaking changes to OIDC trusted-publishing surface would silently break the next release. Patch-grade fix is `npm@^11.5.1`. Revisit post-v1.0.0 alongside the broader supply-chain hardening pass.
- **Story 5.2 needs a one-time hand-bump path to start the v1.0.0 prerelease cycle** ‚Äî `.github/workflows/release.yaml:70-75`. Current `version_bump: alpha|beta|rc` logic uses `npm version prerelease --preid=...` which produces `0.10.1-alpha.0` from `0.10.0` ‚Äî NOT `1.0.0-alpha.0`. To cut `v1.0.0-rc.1` (Story 5.2's plan), a one-time manual bump is needed first: `npm version 1.0.0-rc.0 --no-git-tag-version`, commit-and-push-to-main via PR, then dispatch `release.yaml` with `version_bump: rc` (which will increment to `1.0.0-rc.1`). Alternatively, extend `release.yaml` with `premajor-rc` choice option ‚Äî but that bloats workflow surface for a one-time event. **Action for Story 5.1 (readiness audit):** decide between hand-bump procedure (documented in `docs/RELEASING.md ¬ß Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1`) and workflow extension. Recommendation: hand-bump (lighter surface, one-time event). Tracked from Story 3.1 code review.

lines: - **`## [Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle after first release is unverified** ‚Äî `CHANGELOG.md:5`. Path (i) pre-inserted the anchor for Story 3.2's first cut, but `conventional-changelog-cli`'s behavior on the SECOND release (does it preserve, replace, or duplicate the heading?) is preset-specific and unverified. Story 3.2's alpha cut is the explicit live validator for this; Story 3.2's review will surface concrete behavior and decide whether a re-insert step is needed in `release.yaml`.

- **Third-party actions (`softprops/action-gh-release@v2`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`) not pinned to SHA** ‚Äî `.github/workflows/release.yaml:55, 158`. Floating major-version tags. With `id-token: write` + `contents: write`, a compromised maintainer or compromised tag could exfiltrate. Industry best practice is SHA-pinning; not specified in the spec; consistent with `quality.yaml` and `publish.yaml` patterns elsewhere in the repo. Post-v1.0.0 hardening pass ‚Äî likely Story 5.1 audit or a dedicated supply-chain story.

- **`npm install -g npm@latest` is unpinned ‚Äî future npm major can break OIDC contract silently** ‚Äî `.github/workflows/release.yaml:52`. AC #4 / FR13 specifies "npm CLI ‚â• 11.5.1" but the implementation pins to `latest`. Matches the research doc worked example, so consistent with the design baseline. A future `npm@12.x` with breaking changes to OIDC trusted-publishing surface would silently break the next release. Patch-grade fix is `npm@^11.5.1`. Revisit post-v1.0.0 alongside the broader supply-chain hardening pass.

+- **Story 5.2 needs a one-time hand-bump path to start the v1.0.0 prerelease cycle** ‚Äî `.github/workflows/release.yaml:70-75`. Current `version_bump: alpha|beta|rc` logic uses `npm version prerelease --preid=...` which produces `0.10.1-alpha.0` from `0.10.0` ‚Äî NOT `1.0.0-alpha.0`. To cut `v1.0.0-rc.1` (Story 5.2's plan), a one-time manual bump is needed first: `npm version 1.0.0-rc.0 --no-git-tag-version`, commit-and-push-to-main via PR, then dispatch `release.yaml` with `version_bump: rc` (which will increment to `1.0.0-rc.1`). Alternatively, extend `release.yaml` with `premajor-rc` choice option ‚Äî but that bloats workflow surface for a one-time event. **Action for Story 5.1 (readiness audit):** decide between hand-bump procedure (documented in `docs/RELEASING.md ¬ß Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1`) and workflow extension. Recommendation: hand-bump (lighter surface, one-time event). Tracked from Story 3.1 code review.

Reply with the number.

name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` ‚Äî the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md ¬ß npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -E 'closes \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric 'closes [#...]' refs ‚Äî aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## üéâ Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs ‚Äî aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: npm publish --dry-run

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## üéâ Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

} >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

name: Quality & Validation

# Runs comprehensive quality checks on all PRs:
# - Prettier (formatting)
# - ESLint (linting)
# - markdownlint (markdown quality)
# - Schema validation (YAML structure)
# - Agent schema tests (fixture-based validation)
# - Installation component tests (CLI + compilation)
# - Workflow state consistency tests
# - Knowledge base tests
# - Skill structure validation
# - File reference validation
# - Python script tests (pytest via uv)

"on":
  pull_request:
    branches: ["**"]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  prettier:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Prettier format check
        run: npm run format:check

  eslint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: ESLint
        run: npm run lint

  markdownlint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: markdownlint
        run: npm run lint:md

  validate:
    # Matrix Linux + Windows to catch platform-divergence bugs (path handling,
    # line endings, shell scripts). macOS skipped: POSIX-equivalent to Linux
    # for SKF's Node + Python surface area ‚Äî add only if a Mac-only bug surfaces.
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Smoke test conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution (Windows path/EOL check)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        run: npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --help

      - name: Validate YAML schemas
        run: npm run validate:schemas

      - name: Run agent schema validation tests
        run: npm run test:schemas

      - name: Test installation components
        run: npm run test:install

      - name: Test CLI integration
        run: npm run test:cli

      - name: Test workflow state consistency
        run: npm run test:workflow

      - name: Test knowledge base
        run: npm run test:knowledge

      - name: Validate skills structure
        run: npm run validate:skills

      - name: Validate file references
        run: npm run validate:refs

  python:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run Python tests
        run: npm run test:python

input.old_string:
- [ ] [Review][Patch] **Gate `git push origin HEAD:main` on `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`** [release.yaml:143-144] ‚Äî Workflow unconditionally pushes HEAD to `main`. Story 3.2's alpha cut dispatches from `feat/release-workflow`, whose HEAD is NOT a fast-forward of `main` ‚Äî push will be rejected by branch protection (required PR reviews per Epic 1 recon), but only AFTER the immutable tag has been pushed (line 140) and BEFORE npm publish runs (line 146). Result: split-brain (tag exists, main not updated, npm not published). **Resolution:** Add `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` to the "Push commit to main" step plus an else-branch logging step so Story 3.2's reviewer sees clear expected-skip behavior in the logs. Captures the actual invariant ("only advance main when running from main") without coupling to prerelease semantics. (Originally D1.)
- [ ] [Review][Patch] **Add `npm publish --dry-run` step before tag push to catch pre-publish failures early** [release.yaml:91-134] ‚Äî Current order: commit ‚Üí push tag (immutable) ‚Üí push main ‚Üí npm publish ‚Üí GH release. If publish fails, tag+main-push succeeded but no npm artifact (split-brain). **Resolution:** Insert a `npm publish --dry-run` step between "Assert no bogus issue refs" and "Commit version bump" ‚Äî runs against the bumped tree before any immutable side-effect, catches the common pre-publish failure classes (file-list, package.json validation, manifest issues). OIDC-auth failures still slip through but are transient/retryable and don't leave split-brain (publish fails ‚Üí tag still owns the version ‚Üí re-dispatch retries publish). Preserves the documented tag-first ordering convention from `manual-release.yaml` and the research doc. (Originally D2.)

- [x] [Review][Defer] **`npm version prerelease` cannot start a new major prerelease cycle** [release.yaml:70-75] ‚Äî Current bump logic produces `0.10.1-alpha.0` from `0.10.0` ‚Äî NOT `1.0.0-alpha.0`. Story 5.2's `v1.0.0-rc.1` cut needs a one-time hand-bump (`npm version 1.0.0-rc.0 --no-git-tag-version`) followed by a normal `rc` dispatch. Adding `premajor-*` inputs to `release.yaml` would bloat surface for a one-time event. **Deferred to Story 5.1 readiness audit** ‚Äî that story owns v1.0.0 cycle planning and is the natural home for the procedure (or a new `docs/RELEASING.md ¬ß Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` block). (Originally D3.)

Misses `Closes [#‚Ä¶`, `fixes [#‚Ä¶`, `Fixes [#‚Ä¶`, `resolves [#‚Ä¶`, `Resolves [#‚Ä¶` ‚Äî all common conventional-commits footer forms that the same preset would render. Same class of bug Story 2.2's review caught by hand. Extend regex to `(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]` with `grep -iE` (case-insensitive). Two-character change, same false-positive surface, much wider coverage.

Two-character change, same false-positive surface, much wider coverage.
- [ ] [Review][Patch] **Hardcoded GitHub repo URL ‚Äî should use `github.server_url`/`github.repository`** [release.yaml:125, 172] ‚Äî Two hardcoded `https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge` strings: in the release-notes "Full Changelog" footer (line 125) and the step summary GitHub URL (line 172). If the repo is renamed or transferred, both produce dead links. Replace with `${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}` ‚Äî standard GitHub Actions context expression, zero runtime cost.
- [ ] [Review][Patch] **Windows smoke step's `--no-install --help` doesn't exercise the `tempfile@5` codepath** [quality.yaml:98-100] ‚Äî AC #16 framing claims the smoke step surfaces "tempfile@5-class Windows path/EOL breakage." `--help` resolves the bin and prints usage ‚Äî does NOT write any temp files. The smoke catches bin-resolution failures (still useful) but not the specific class the spec/Dev Notes name. Either rename the step ("Smoke test conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution on Windows") to match what it actually does, OR upgrade the invocation to one that exercises tempfile (e.g., `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits --output-unreleased > /dev/null` against a synthetic minimal repo state ‚Äî adds setup complexity). Renaming is the cheap fix; tempfile-exercising smoke is post-v1.0.0 hardening.

- [ ] [Review][Patch] **Hardcoded GitHub repo URL ‚Äî should use `github.server_url`/`github.repository`** [release.yaml:125, 172] ‚Äî Two hardcoded `https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge` strings: in the release-notes "Full Changelog" footer (line 125) and the step summary GitHub URL (line 172). If the repo is renamed or transferred, both produce dead links. Replace with `${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}` ‚Äî standard GitHub Actions context expression, zero runtime cost.
- [ ] [Review][Patch] **Windows smoke step's `--no-install --help` doesn't exercise the `tempfile@5` codepath** [quality.yaml:98-100] ‚Äî AC #16 framing claims the smoke step surfaces "tempfile@5-class Windows path/EOL breakage." `--help` resolves the bin and prints usage ‚Äî does NOT write any temp files. Either rename the step ("Smoke test conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution on Windows") to match what it actually does, OR upgrade the invocation to one that exercises tempfile (e.g., `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits --output-unreleased > /dev/null` against a synthetic minimal repo state ‚Äî adds setup complexity). Renaming is the cheap fix; tempfile-exercising smoke is post-v1.0.0 hardening.

lines: - [x] [Review][Patch] **Add `npm publish --dry-run` step before tag push to catch pre-publish failures early** [release.yaml:98-101] ‚Äî APPLIED. Inserted "Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)" step between "Assert no bogus issue refs" and "Commit version bump". Runs `npm publish --dry-run` against the bumped tree with `NPM_TOKEN: ""` ‚Äî catches file-list, package.json validation, and manifest issues before any immutable side-effect. Preserves the documented tag-first ordering convention from `manual-release.yaml`. (Originally D2.)

- [x] [Review][Defer] **`npm version prerelease` cannot start a new major prerelease cycle** [release.yaml:70-75] ‚Äî Current bump logic produces `0.10.1-alpha.0` from `0.10.0` ‚Äî NOT `1.0.0-alpha.0`. Story 5.2's `v1.0.0-rc.1` cut needs a one-time hand-bump (`npm version 1.0.0-rc.0 --no-git-tag-version`) followed by a normal `rc` dispatch. Adding `premajor-*` inputs to `release.yaml` would bloat surface for a one-time event. **Deferred to Story 5.1 readiness audit** ‚Äî that story owns v1.0.0 cycle planning and is the natural home for the procedure (or a new `docs/RELEASING.md ¬ß Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` block). commit 0a19b1adcb27a4086231bbaa77acea327d937415
Author:     armel <armelhbobdad@gmail.com>
AuthorDate: Tue Apr 21 06:12:49 2026 +0400
Commit:     armel <armelhbobdad@gmail.com>
CommitDate: Tue Apr 21 06:12:49 2026 +0400

    docs(stability): address code-review findings (18 patches)
    
    Parallel adversarial code review of `2fc1069` (Blind Hunter + Edge Case
    Hunter + Acceptance Auditor) surfaced 18 patches plus 5 decision-needed
    items (all resolved in-story) and 6 defer entries (logged to
    `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md`). Acceptance
    Auditor returned no AC violations; findings were contract-precision and
    documentation-fidelity issues.
    
    Substantive changes:
    
    - New `### Engine & Platform Compatibility` subsection commits Node.js
      support tracking `package.json` `engines.node` (currently `>=22.0.0`)
      and Linux/macOS/Windows OS support, closing a gap in the v1.0.0
      surface (D4).
    - Added `.gitignore` to the install-layout list ‚Äî `tools/cli/lib/installer.js`
      appends `_bmad/_memory/` at install time, previously uncovered (P1).
    - Absolute GitHub URLs for `CHANGELOG.md`, `platform-codes.yaml`, and
      `test/schema/agent.js` in ¬ß References so links resolve for consumers
      reading from the npm tarball (where `.npmignore` excludes those paths);
      `docs/RELEASING.md` stays relative as a same-folder sibling (D1).
    - Carved out `test/schema/**` from the `test/` @internal bullet ‚Äî the
      zod schema is authoritative for the manifest contract, so the @internal
      wholesale exclusion needed narrowing (D3).
    - Softened `skf update` state-preservation commitment from invariant to
      best-effort with CHANGELOG.md escalation for breaking migrations ‚Äî
      sidecar file formats remain @internal, so the prior wording over-
      promised (D5).
    - Clarified npx wrapper indirection (`tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js` execs
      `skf-cli.js`); user-cancel surface for `skf install` (Ctrl-C or
      decline); `skf update` "nothing to do" exit-0 path; `manifest.version`
      field semantics; `-h/--help` availability per-subcommand;
      `platforms.<ide>.installer.target_dir` carve-out in
      `platform-codes.yaml`; `module` field consumer-authoring expectation
      vs SKF-injected; zod snippet labeled "simplified" with pointer to
      live source; GitHub Issues URL for the contract-breakage report path.
    - Reworded Installation Layout intro ("exactly these paths" ‚Üí "the
      following paths, some names configurable at install time") and the
      forge-sidecar bullet to stop conflating covered (directory) vs
      @internal (file formats) vs runtime-preserved (update round-trip).
    - Replaced the "intermediate exit codes not part of the contract" line
      with an additive-on-minor commitment for future codes beyond 0/1.
    
    Quality gates re-run green:
    
    - `npm test` ‚Äî all 12 subcommands (test:schemas, test:install, test:cli,
      test:workflow, test:python, test:knowledge, validate:schemas,
      validate:skills, validate:refs, lint, lint:md, format:check)
    - `npm run docs:validate-links` ‚Äî 16 files, 0 issues
    - `validate:refs` ‚Äî 269 references, 180 files, 0 broken
    
    Scope: docs/STABILITY.md only (+23 / -18, net +5 lines). No
    package.json, workflow, source, or test changes. DRAFT banner
    preserved ‚Äî Story 5.1 pre-flight audit still owns the final lock.
    
    Deferred (6 items in `deferred-work.md`): Starlight sidebar entry for
    STABILITY.md (D2, Story 5.1 handoff); `commands/install.js` silent-
    exit-0 on `{success: false}` (code fix); `package.json main` pointing
    at side-effectful CLI (pre-v1.0.0 housekeeping); RFC 2119 keyword
    conventions (prose hygiene); SKILL.md `description` trigger-phrase
    grammar (upstream agentskills.io territory); caret-range guidance
    pre-v1.0.0 (Story 5.1 lock flip).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: a116ef552cc6

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml .github/workflows/quality.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): address code-review findings (5 patches)

Parallel adversarial code review of `1295bf6` (Blind Hunter + Edge Case
Hunter + Acceptance Auditor) surfaced 5 patches plus 3 decision-needed
items (all resolved in-story ‚Äî 2 became patches, 1 deferred to Story
5.1) and 6 defer entries (logged to `deferred-work.md`). Acceptance
Auditor returned no AC violations; all findings were operational-safety
improvements.

Substantive changes:

- Gate `git push origin HEAD:main` on `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`
  with sibling notice-emitting else-step. Story 3.2's alpha cut from
  `feat/release-workflow` will now cleanly skip the main push (tag
  pushed; main correctly NOT advanced) instead of split-braining at
  branch-protection rejection. (Originally D1.)

- Add `npm publish --dry-run` step between the bogus-ref assertion and
  the commit step. Catches file-list, package.json validation, and
  manifest issues BEFORE any immutable side-effect (tag push, npm
  publish). Preserves the documented tag-first ordering convention
  inherited from `manual-release.yaml` and the research doc worked
  example. (Originally D2.)

- Widen bogus-issue-ref grep to `(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]`
  with `-i` (case-insensitive). Original lowercase-`closes`-only
  pattern would have missed the same class of bug under capitalized
  or alternative-verb conventional-commits footer forms ‚Äî exactly the
  kind of preset false-positive Story 2.2's review caught by hand.

- Replace hardcoded `https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge`
  in two places (release-notes "Full Changelog" footer + step-summary
  GitHub URL) with `${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}`.
  Repo rename/transfer no longer breaks links.

- Rename `quality.yaml` Windows smoke step from "Smoke test
  conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution (Windows path/EOL check)"
  to "Smoke test conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution on Windows".
  `npx --no-install ... --help` exercises bin resolution, NOT the
  `tempfile@5` codepath the original framing claimed.

Quality gates re-run green:

- `prettier --check` on both files
- `eslint --max-warnings=0` on both files
- `validate:schemas`

Scope: `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (+18 / -3) +
`.github/workflows/quality.yaml` (+1 / -1, rename only). No source,
test, docs, package.json, or other workflow changes piggybacked.

Deferred (6 items in `deferred-work.md`): `publish.yaml` `v*` tag
trigger (Story 3.3); marketplace.json sed multi-version-field risk
(Story 5.1); `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle after first release
(Story 3.2 alpha cut validates); third-party action SHA pinning
(post-v1.0.0); `npm@latest` pin (post-v1.0.0); premajor prerelease
path for v1.0.0 cut (Story 5.1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Stage and commit the 5 code-review patches
